---
title: "Model fitting and inference for infectious disease dynamics"
site: distill::distill_website
comments: false
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: chat-fill
      text: Dive into the course
      href: sessions/getting-started.html
    - icon: github
      text: Visit us on GitHub
      href: https://github.com/sbfnk/mfiidd.julia
---

A course and living resource for learning about model fitting and inference for infectious disease dynamics.

## Prerequisites

To get the most from this course, you should be comfortable with:

- **Basic programming**: Variables, functions, loops, conditionals (in any language)
- **Probability**: Distributions, Bayes' theorem, conditional probability
- **Calculus**: Derivatives, integrals (helpful but not essential)
- **Epidemiology basics**: Compartmental models (S, I, R states), transmission dynamics

No prior Julia experience is required—the Getting Started session introduces Julia syntax as you go.

## Session dependencies

Sessions should be completed in order within each tier:

| Session | Prerequisites |
|---------|--------------|
| Getting Started | None |
| Introduction | Getting Started |
| MCMC | Introduction |
| MCMC Diagnostics | MCMC |
| Model Checking | MCMC Diagnostics |
| SEITL Interlude | Model Checking |
| Particle Filters | SEITL Interlude |
| Particle MCMC | Particle Filters |
| Observation Models | Particle MCMC |
| Variational Inference | MCMC (can skip stochastic sessions) |
| Universal DEs | Introduction (independent track) |

We invite anyone to use the materials here in their own teaching and learning. For questions and discussion of the course or its content, we welcome all users to the [Discussion board](https://github.com/sbfnk/mfiidd.julia/discussions). We welcome all forms of [contributions, additions and suggestions](https://github.com/sbfnk/mfiidd.julia/issues) for improving the materials.

All materials here are provided under the permissive [MIT License](https://github.com/sbfnk/mfiidd.julia/blob/main/LICENSE).
